name: rhode (nymph of rhodes)
shortDescription: Rhode was a nymph and the personification of the island of Rhodes in Greek mythology. She was a daughter of Poseidon and Halia, and her union with Helios resulted in the birth of the Heliadae, the first inhabitants of Rhodes.
description: Rhode, in Greek mythology, was a nymph and the divine personification of the island of Rhodes. She was the daughter of Poseidon, god of the sea, and Halia. According to myth, when the gods divided the earth amongst themselves, Helios, the sun god, was absent. When he returned, the land had already been distributed. However, Helios saw a beautiful island rising from the sea and claimed it for himself, naming it Rhodes after the nymph Rhode. Helios shone upon the island, endowing it with warmth and fertility. Rhode's union with Helios resulted in the birth of seven sons known as the Heliadae, who were skilled in various arts and sciences and became the first inhabitants of Rhodes. These sons were Ochimus, Cercaphus, Macar, Actis, Triopas, Candalus, and Tenages. The island of Rhodes was sacred to Helios, and the Colossus of Rhodes, one of the Seven Wonders of the Ancient World, was erected in his honor. Rhode's story highlights the connection between the divine and the natural world, explaining the origin and prosperity of the island of Rhodes through the union of the sun god and a sea nymph.
